title: Fix MongoDB Projection to Ignore Embedded Fields

statement.markdown: Implement a MongoDB projection that ignores embedded fields. Given a MongoDB document and a projection object, return the filtered document. The projection object specifies which fields to include or exclude from the result. If a field is not specified in the projection, it should be included by default. Embedded fields are represented as nested objects in the document.
statement.notes: The projection object can have the following formats: { field: true, field: false } or { field: { subfield: true } }

constraints.description: Maximum number of fields in the document
Maximum number of fields in the projection

examples.explanation: The age field is excluded from the result since it's set to false in the projection.
The city field in the address object is excluded from the result since it's not specified in the projection.

hints.text: Start by parsing the input document and projection as JSON objects.
Iterate over the fields in the document and check if they are specified in the projection.
If a field is specified in the projection, include it in the result. If a field is not specified, include it by default.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing MongoDB Projection
editorial.intuition: The goal is to filter the document based on the projection object. We can achieve this by iterating over the fields in the document and checking if they are specified in the projection.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use a recursive approach to handle embedded fields. However, this may lead to performance issues for large documents.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a simple iterative approach to handle both top-level fields and embedded fields.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a recursive approach to handle embedded fields and a simple iterative approach to handle top-level fields.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The solution correctly filters the document based on the projection object.
editorial.dryRun: Example run: Given the document {name: John, age: 30, address: {street: 123 Main St, city: Anytown}} and the projection {name: true, address: {street: true}}, the solution returns {name: John, address: {street: 123 Main St}}.
commonMistakes: Failing to include fields not specified in the projection
Including embedded fields that are not specified in the projection
